approval_request.minimum_next_approval_scope: A human must approve the exact Fiverr account route, seller terms, gig copy, package scope, pricing/refund/revision policy, payout/tax/payment assumptions, client-data and credential policy, IP/license claims, platform/no-guarantee wording, and public listing before any external action.

next_local_action: If the operator approves this packet, create an exact-scope service request for one Fiverr account/gig copy review. Do not log in, create or edit a seller profile, create/edit/publish a gig, message buyers, accept orders, accept terms, touch payout/tax setup, or perform any public action from this packet alone.
pre_publish_checklist: Verify the gig copy makes no fake seller history, reviews, earnings, ranking, conversion, or platform-approval claims.
Verify the deliverables are local Markdown/templates only and do not imply live browser automation or production integration.
Verify client inputs exclude credentials, private systems, account access, regulated data, and sensitive personal data.
Verify pricing, refund, revision, support, cancellation, payout, tax, and Fiverr seller terms are reviewed by the human operator.
Verify all source assets are owned, licensed, or clearly original enough to sell as a service deliverable.
Verify the public listing title, package tiers, FAQ, requirements, and gallery assets are approved exactly before publishing.

selected_bundle.buyer: AI builders or operators with a repeatable workflow they want to make safer and reusable.
not_in_scope: No credentials, APIs, client private data, or public posting.
No revenue, compliance, or platform-approval guarantee.
scope: Map one workflow from plain-language notes or local sample material.
Add allowed/forbidden actions, stop gates, artifact contract, and acceptance checks.
selected_bundle.service_name_local: Package one AI-agent workflow into a reusable skill packet
selected_bundle.turnaround_hypothesis: 2-4 business days after receiving non-sensitive workflow notes.
selected_bundle.why_first: It reuses the strongest existing digital product asset, has clear local deliverables, and avoids live integrations, client credentials, or production system access.
